text: Some transfer of training effects on VO2max exists between different exercise modes (cycling, running, swimming), but cross-training effects never exceed those induced by sport-specific training.
Running as a cross-training mode produces more noticeable transfer of VO2max training effects to other sports compared to cycling or swimming.
Swim training results in minimal transfer of VO2max training effects to other exercise modes.
The principle of training specificity has greater significance for highly trained athletes, meaning cross-training provides diminishing returns as fitness level increases.
Cross-training may be highly beneficial for the general population in terms of overall fitness improvement.
Cross-training is an appropriate supplement during rehabilitation from physical injury and during periods of overtraining or psychological fatigue.
